response.justification: This lever duplicates [bd43cd39-f2f0-4358-9f5b-e4dbfdccc474] (same name and core purpose). The distinction is minimal: both focus on technical demonstrators influencing policy. The unique review comment about 'Controls Security vs. Convenience' in this lever is subsumed by the more specific [bd43cd39-f2f0-4358-9f5b-e4dbfdccc474]'s structured consequences and metrics.

This lever overlaps significantly with [80b177d0-c67e-4bc2-bd50-3f49b815e633] (same name and core purpose). Both focus on policy advocacy shaping procurement, but [80b177d0] includes specific metrics (20% likelihood of influencing procurement) and clearer alignment with AltID. The unique review comment about 'Speed vs. Thoroughness' here is subsumed by [80b177d0]'s structured consequences and institutional engagement focus.

The unique 'Scope vs. Focus' review comment here is subsumed by [1731ad9a]'s structured consequences and institutional engagement focus.

This lever duplicates [f1c0d856-0cfc-4def-ab68-c58bb8b17551] (same name and core purpose). Both focus on procurement intervention to enforce platform neutrality, with nearly identical consequences and options. The unique 'Cost vs. Quality' review comment here is subsumed by the broader scope of [f1c0d856], which includes specific metrics like 'platform-neutral language included in AltID documents' and clearer alignment with procurement processes.

This lever duplicates [db44b7de-1e33-4a98-8e11-49405e76a45a] (same name and core purpose). This lever is distinct from others due to its specific metrics (15% increased policy traction) and clear alignment with procurement credibility. While overlapping with [a02b023d-b164-4b26-ae7d-b033d88b93fc] and [1b3c2ca2-01c4-4785-91cb-59dc55af3fd0], it provides more granular outcomes and structured consequences. deduplicated_levers.name: Technical Feasibility Strategy
deduplicated_levers.consequences: Demonstrating technical feasibility impacts policy. Immediate: Demonstrators built → Systemic: 15% increased policy traction due to concrete evidence → Strategic: Enhanced credibility in procurement negotiations.
options: Focus demonstrator efforts on a single, highly secure authentication method, such as FIDO2, on a mainstream Android device.
Develop demonstrators for both FIDO2 on GrapheneOS and a browser-based fallback, accepting slightly longer development timelines.
Explore passwordless authentication via blockchain-anchored identity and decentralized identifiers (DIDs) alongside FIDO2 and browser-based fallback demonstrators.
deduplicated_levers.review: Controls Technical Risk vs. Scope. Weakness: The options don't explicitly address the cost implications of each demonstrator approach.
deduplicated_levers.deduplication_justification: This lever is distinct from others due to its specific metrics (15% increased policy traction) and clear alignment with procurement credibility. While overlapping with [a02b023d-b164-4b26-ae7d-b033d88b93fc] and [1b3c2ca2-01c4-4785-91cb-59dc55af3fd0], it provides more granular outcomes and structured consequences. The unique 'Technical Risk vs. Scope' review comment here adds specificity not fully captured in other duplicates.

options: Focus on including general language about supplier concentration risk in AltID procurement documents.
Advocate for specific requirements for platform-neutral access and certified fallback authentication paths.

deduplicated_levers.consequences: Demonstrating viable alternatives impacts adoption. Immediate: Increased confidence in platform-neutral options → Systemic: 15% faster adoption through proven feasibility → Strategic: Strengthened negotiating position with vendors and regulators.
options: Focus demonstrator efforts on a single, well-defined use case within AltID, prioritizing security and user experience over broad feature coverage.

deduplicated_levers.name: EU Standards Engagement Strategy
deduplicated_levers.consequences: EU standards engagement ensures long-term impact. Immediate: Alignment of Danish advocacy with broader European requirements → Systemic: 10% faster adoption of platform-neutral standards across Europe → Strategic: Increased interoperability and reduced platform lock-in for Danish digital identity solutions.
options: Monitor relevant EU bodies and technical working groups connected to the European Digital Identity Wallet framework, providing input and feedback on draft standards and regulations.

Actively participate in EU-level discussions on interoperability, portability, resilience, and reduced platform lock-in, advocating for the inclusion of platform-neutral access and certified fallback mechanisms.

Propose and champion specific technical standards and protocols that promote platform-neutrality and fallback access within the European Digital Identity Wallet framework, leveraging decentralized identity (DID) and verifiable credential (VC) technologies.

While overlapping with [2e9016aa], it provides more granular outcomes and addresses 'Scope of Influence vs. Resource Allocation' in its review. The unique emphasis on technical standardization and interoperability justifies retaining it separately.

system_prompt:
Evaluate each of the provided strategic levers individually. Classify every lever explicitly into one of:

- keep: Lever is distinct, unique, and essential.
- absorb: Lever overlaps significantly with another lever. Explicitly state the lever ID it should be merged into.
- remove: Lever is fully redundant. Removing it loses no meaningful detail. Use this sparingly.

Provide concise, explicit justifications mentioning lever IDs clearly. Always prefer "absorb" over "remove" to retain important details.

Always provide a justification for the classification. Explain why the lever is distinct from others. Don't use the same uninformative boilerplate.

Respect Hierarchy: When absorbing, merge the more specific lever into the more general one.
Don't take the more general lever and absorb it into a narrower one.
Also compare a lever against the group of already-merged levers.

Use "keep" if you lack understanding of what the lever is doing. This way a potential important lever is not getting removed.
Describe what the issue is in the justification.

Don't play it too safe, so you fail to perform the core task: consolidate the levers and get rid of the duplicates.

You must classify and justify **every lever** provided in the input.
